title: Two Pointers
description: Use two indices moving toward each other or in the same direction on sorted arrays or strings.

useCases.title: Merge sorted log streams
useCases.description: When merging two sorted audit logs by timestamp, two pointers walk each stream without re-sorting.
useCases.techExample: Used in log aggregation pipelines (Splunk, ELK) when combining sorted shard outputs.

useCases.title: Deduplicate sorted IDs
useCases.description: Database cursors over sorted primary keys use two-pointer logic to find unique vs duplicate ranges.
useCases.techExample: Batch dedup in ETL jobs reading sorted CSV exports from relational DBs.

solutions.title: Brute Force — Check All Pairs
solutions.explanation: Try every pair (i, j) and check if numbers[i] + numbers[j] == target.

solutions.title: Two Pointers — Optimal
solutions.explanation: Place left at start and right at end. If sum is too small, move left right; if too large, move right left.

solutions.title: Brute Force — Three Nested Loops
solutions.explanation: Check all triplets (i, j, k) where i < j < k.

solutions.title: Sort + Two Pointers
solutions.explanation: Sort array. Fix index i, then use two pointers on the remaining subarray to find pairs summing to -nums[i].

title: Sliding Window
description: Maintain a window over contiguous subarrays/substrings and slide it while tracking constraints.

useCases.title: Rate limiting
useCases.description: Sliding window counters track requests in the last N seconds for API throttling.
useCases.techExample: Redis INCR with TTL or token bucket implementations in API gateways.

useCases.title: Streaming analytics
useCases.description: Compute rolling averages or counts over the last K events in real-time pipelines.
useCases.techExample: Kafka windowed aggregations and Flink tumbling/sliding windows.

solutions.title: Brute Force — Check All Substrings
solutions.explanation: Generate every substring and check if all characters are unique.

solutions.title: Sliding Window with Hash Map
solutions.explanation: Expand window with right pointer. If duplicate found, shrink from left until duplicate is removed.

title: Fast & Slow Pointers
description: Two pointers moving at different speeds for cycle detection and middle-finding.

useCases.title: Distributed cycle detection
useCases.description: Floyd's algorithm detects cycles in linked structures — analogous to deadlock detection in wait-for graphs.
useCases.techExample: JVM thread dump analysis and resource leak detection in long-running services.

questions.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List

title: Merge Intervals
description: Overlapping ranges, scheduling, and sweep-line style problems.

useCases.title: Calendar scheduling
useCases.description: Meeting room allocation merges overlapping time intervals to find free slots.
useCases.techExample: Google Calendar, Outlook, and Calendly use interval merging for conflict detection.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons

title: Cyclic Sort
description: Array contains numbers in range [1..n]; find missing, duplicate, or misplaced elements.

useCases.title: Slot allocation
useCases.description: Assign items to fixed slots when IDs are in a known range without extra memory.
useCases.techExample: Memory pool allocators and disk block remapping in storage engines.

questions.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
Find All Duplicates in an Array

title: In-place Linked List Reversal
description: Reverse full list, a segment, or k-group in O(1) extra space.

useCases.title: Undo/redo buffers
useCases.description: Reversing linked structures models stack-like undo chains in editors.
useCases.techExample: Text editor undo stacks and browser history back-navigation.

title: Tree BFS
description: Level-by-level traversal using a queue; shortest path in unweighted trees.

useCases.title: Org chart traversal
useCases.description: BFS walks an org tree level by level for hierarchy displays.
useCases.techExample: LinkedIn org charts, LDAP directory browsers, and file-system tree UIs.

questions.title: Binary Tree Zigzag Level Order Traversal
Average of Levels in Binary Tree
Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node
Binary Tree Level Order Traversal II

title: Tree DFS
description: Recursive or stack-based depth-first traversal for path and subtree problems.

useCases.title: File system traversal
useCases.description: DFS walks directory trees to compute sizes, find files, or build indexes.
useCases.techExample: Unix find, ripgrep directory walks, and S3 prefix listing.

questions.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a BST

title: Two Heaps
description: Balance two priority queues to track running median or similar streaming stats.

useCases.title: Real-time percentile metrics
useCases.description: Two heaps maintain median/percentile over streaming latency samples.
useCases.techExample: Datadog, Prometheus histograms, and APM latency dashboards.

title: Subsets / Combinations
description: Generate all subsets, combinations, and permutations with backtracking.

useCases.title: Feature flag combinations
useCases.description: Enumerate all subset combinations of feature toggles for A/B test configs.
useCases.techExample: LaunchDarkly flag targeting and experiment matrix generation.

questions.title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number

title: Modified Binary Search
description: Binary search on sorted/rotated arrays, first/last occurrence, or answer space.

useCases.title: Database index lookups
useCases.description: B-tree indexes use binary search to locate rows in O(log n).
useCases.techExample: PostgreSQL and MySQL index scans on sorted B-tree pages.

questions.title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array
Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array

title: Top K Elements
description: Find K largest, smallest, or most frequent elements using heap or bucket sort.

useCases.title: Top-N query optimization
useCases.description: Databases use heap-based top-K for ORDER BY ... LIMIT K without full sort.
useCases.techExample: Elasticsearch top hits aggregations and SQL LIMIT with priority queues.

questions.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
Find K Pairs with Smallest Sums

title: K-way Merge
description: Merge K sorted lists or arrays efficiently using a min-heap.

useCases.title: Merge sorted shard results
useCases.description: Distributed databases merge sorted results from multiple shards.
useCases.techExample: Elasticsearch multi-shard search result merging and MapReduce combiners.

questions.title: Kth Smallest Element in a Sorted Matrix
Smallest Range Covering Elements from K Lists

title: Monotonic Stack
description: Stack maintaining monotonic order for next greater/smaller and histogram problems.

useCases.title: Stock span / price alerts
useCases.description: Monotonic stacks compute how long prices stay below a threshold.
useCases.techExample: Real-time trading dashboards computing support/resistance levels.

title: Monotonic Queue / Deque
description: Deque maintaining monotonic order for sliding window max/min in O(n).

useCases.title: Rolling max latency
useCases.description: Track maximum API latency over a sliding time window in O(1) amortized.
useCases.techExample: CDN edge monitoring and real-time anomaly detection pipelines.

questions.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K

title: Prefix Sum + Hash Map
description: Prefix sums with hash map for subarray sum and count problems.

useCases.title: Billing period aggregation
useCases.description: Prefix sums compute cumulative usage over time windows for metered billing.
useCases.techExample: AWS CloudWatch metric rollups and Stripe usage-based billing.

title: Hash Map / Set
description: Frequency counting, anagrams, deduplication, and O(1) lookups.

useCases.title: In-memory caching
useCases.description: Hash maps power O(1) key-value lookups in caches and session stores.
useCases.techExample: Redis hash tables, Memcached, and LRU cache implementations.

title: Backtracking
description: Explore all valid configurations with pruning and undo steps.

useCases.title: Constraint solvers
useCases.description: Backtracking explores solution spaces with pruning — used in schedulers and puzzle solvers.
useCases.techExample: Kubernetes pod scheduling constraints and SAT solvers.

title: Graph BFS / DFS
description: Connected components, islands, clone graph, and shortest path in grids.

useCases.title: Social graph traversal
useCases.description: BFS finds shortest connection paths; DFS explores friend networks.
useCases.techExample: LinkedIn 'people you may know' and Facebook friend suggestions.

title: Topological Sort
description: Dependency ordering for DAGs — courses, build order, alien dictionary.

useCases.title: Build system task ordering
useCases.description: Topological sort determines compilation order respecting dependencies.
useCases.techExample: Bazel, Gradle, and npm dependency resolution graphs.

title: Union Find (Disjoint Set)
description: Dynamic connectivity, cycle detection, and grouping with path compression.

useCases.title: Network connectivity
useCases.description: Union-find tracks connected components as edges are added dynamically.
useCases.techExample: Kubernetes network policy grouping and Kruskal's MST algorithm.

questions.title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
Most Stones Removed with Same Row or Column

title: Trie
description: Prefix tree for autocomplete, word search, and XOR problems.

useCases.title: Autocomplete / typeahead
useCases.description: Tries power search-as-you-type in browsers, IDEs, and search bars.
useCases.techExample: Google Search autocomplete, VS Code IntelliSense, and Elasticsearch suggesters.

questions.title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array

title: Greedy
description: Local optimal choices that lead to global optimum when structure allows.

useCases.title: Task scheduling
useCases.description: Greedy picks highest-priority tasks first when optimal scheduling applies.
useCases.techExample: OS process schedulers, cron job ordering, and CDN cache eviction (LRU).

title: Bit Manipulation / XOR
description: Bitwise ops, XOR tricks, and subset enumeration via bitmask.

useCases.title: Feature flags & permissions
useCases.description: Bitmasks encode compact permission sets and feature toggles.
useCases.techExample: Unix file permissions (chmod), Linux capabilities, and RBAC bitfields.

title: 1D Dynamic Programming
description: Optimal substructure along a single sequence or linear state.

useCases.title: Resource allocation
useCases.description: DP optimizes sequential decisions with overlapping subproblems.
useCases.techExample: Ad budget allocation, inventory replenishment, and pricing engines.

title: 2D / Grid DP
description: Paths, min cost, and grid-based optimization with 2D state.

useCases.title: Sequence alignment
useCases.description: Edit distance DP aligns strings for diff tools and bioinformatics.
useCases.techExample: Git diff (Myers), DNA sequence alignment, and spell checkers.

title: Knapsack DP
description: Pick items with weight/value constraints; subset sum variants.

useCases.title: Budget optimization
useCases.description: Knapsack models selecting projects/features under budget constraints.
useCases.techExample: Portfolio optimization and cloud resource packing problems.

title: Interval / State Machine DP
description: State transitions over time — buy/sell stock, job scheduling.

useCases.title: Trading strategies
useCases.description: State machine DP models buy/hold/sell decisions over time series.
useCases.techExample: Algorithmic trading backtesting and dynamic pricing engines.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock III

title: Tree DP
description: Optimal decisions on tree nodes — include/exclude child subtrees.

useCases.title: Network node placement
useCases.description: Place monitors/cameras on trees minimizing cost while covering all nodes.
useCases.techExample: CDN edge server placement and network monitoring point selection.

title: Divide & Conquer
description: Split problem in half, merge results — often O(n log n).

useCases.title: Parallel map-reduce
useCases.description: Divide data, process in parallel, merge results — core MapReduce pattern.
useCases.techExample: Hadoop/Spark distributed sorting and aggregation.

title: Stack (General)
description: Parsing, expression evaluation, and nested structure validation.

useCases.title: Expression parsing
useCases.description: Stacks evaluate arithmetic expressions and parse nested JSON/HTML.
useCases.techExample: JavaScript V8 parser, JSON.parse, and template engine compilers.

title: Queue / BFS on Grid
description: Multi-source BFS and shortest path on 0-1 or unweighted grids.

useCases.title: Maze / pathfinding
useCases.description: BFS finds shortest paths in game maps, robots, and navigation.
useCases.techExample: Google Maps routing, game AI pathfinding, and warehouse robots.

title: String Patterns
description: Two pointers on strings, rolling hash, and pattern matching.

useCases.title: Plagiarism / duplicate detection
useCases.description: Rolling hash finds duplicate substrings in documents efficiently.
useCases.techExample: GitHub Copilot dedup, search engine duplicate content detection.
